concerns.text: FR-004 defines 'false details (present only in enhanced/reduced versions)' — if participants view manipulated images, these are REAL details they saw, not false memories. This creates a circular/tautological definition where the manipulation itself determines what counts as 'false memory'.

The false memory measurement design is conceptually inverted. Standard visual false memory paradigms test whether participants remember details from a BASELINE image that were NOT present. Here, 'false' details are defined as those added/removed in manipulation, but participants only see manipulated versions — they cannot distinguish baseline from manipulation.

Repeated-measures ANOVA requires same participants in all conditions, but SC-002 specifies '≥50 per condition' suggesting between-subjects design. This is a methodological mismatch that invalidates the stated analysis plan.

Assumption states 'DRM paradigm adaptations for visual stimuli' — DRM is for semantic/verbal false memory (word lists), not visual detail false memory. This may be a category error requiring different validated paradigms (e.g., misinformation effect, source monitoring paradigms).

SC-002 states 'Statistical power is measured against the planned sample size' — power is calculated BEFORE data collection based on effect size estimates, not measured after. This reverses the causal/temporal relationship and is conceptually incorrect.

Constitution Principle VI requires IRB/ethics approval for human subjects research, but spec contains no mention of ethics compliance, consent procedures, or IRB documentation. This is a fundamental violation for psychology research with human participants.
